categories.name: Verbal Skills
categories.description: Build vocabulary precision, word relationships, sentence logic, grammar accuracy, and verbal reasoning under exam-style pressure.

categories.name: Reading Skills
categories.description: Improve comprehension, main idea detection, detail tracking, and inference from passages.

categories.name: Writing Skills
categories.description: Practice grammar, structure, editing, sentence correction, and concise expression.

categories.name: Logical Reasoning
categories.description: Understand arguments, assumptions, inferences, fallacies, and cause-effect reasoning.

categories.name: Analytical Reasoning
categories.description: Solve conditions, arrangements, comparisons, grouping, and rule-based logic sets.

categories.name: Quantitative Skills
categories.description: Strengthen arithmetic, algebra, ratios, percentages, rates, and problem solving.

categories.name: Data Skills
categories.description: Read tables, charts, graphs, data sufficiency prompts, and trend-based questions.

categories.name: Non-Verbal Reasoning
categories.description: Practice patterns, matrices, spatial reasoning, analogies, and visual sequences.

categories.name: Situational Judgement
categories.description: Evaluate professional scenarios, priorities, ethics, teamwork, and decision quality.

categories.name: Digital & Scientific Reasoning
categories.description: Reason about systems, scientific information, digital logic, and evidence-based claims.

skills.name: Core Verbal Reasoning
skills.description: A professional verbal skills path covering vocabulary, synonyms, antonyms, analogies, word relationships, sentence completion, correction, and meaning logic.

skills.name: Argument Reasoning
skills.description: Learn how claims, evidence, hidden assumptions, and conclusions work together in aptitude arguments.
